description_long: This rifle features a robust, industrial design with a prominent rectangular magazine mounted above the receiver, suggesting a bullpup or unconventional layout. The main body is constructed from dark metal, with a matte finish and visible mechanical components, including a charging handle and exposed bolt assembly. The stock is a skeletonized, wire-frame type with wooden grips at both the rear and fore-end, providing ergonomic support for the user. The barrel is relatively short and thick, extending forward from the main body, and lacks any visible muzzle device. The rifle is equipped with basic iron sights, with the front and rear posts aligned along the top. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a mix of metal and wood elements, suitable for rugged urban environments.
description: A dark metal rifle with a top-mounted box magazine, skeletonized wire-frame stock, wooden grips, and basic iron sights. The design is utilitarian and robust, blending metal and wood for urban use.
description_view_0: The rifle is seen from the front, showing the narrow profile of the barrel and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_1: A full side view reveals the rectangular top-mounted magazine, skeletonized stock, wooden grips, and exposed mechanical components.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the wire-frame stock and the back of the receiver, with the wooden grip clearly visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the magazine, stock, and mechanical details from the other side.
